title: dictamnus albus subsp. dasycarpus
intro: Dictamnus albus subsp. dasycarpus, also known as the hairy or Asian tormentil, is a plant species within the family Gentianaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dictamnus albus subsp. dasycarpus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
